methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Anvil Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Mike Xia and Vijay Pradeep that has raised $6.5M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes a comparable universe of 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public company, though a sufficiently narrow peer set at identical or adjacent stages with comparable valuation tiers does not exist in the available data.

Given the limited peer comparables at seed stage, the stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation technique. Using total capital raised of $6.5M as a proxy base value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier for progression from seed to the next financing stage yields an implied valuation of $16.25M. This reflects the expected increase in company value between rounds absent more granular market data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $14M to $19M, centered on a median of $16M. This range incorporates modest downside and upside variance around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties limiting precision include the absence of same-stage or adjacent-stage robotics peers with sufficient data density to apply peer-median methods, and reliance on a single methodological input. A more robust valuation would require either identification of closer-stage comparables or additional fundamental inputs such as revenue, burn rate, or market traction metrics.
